{{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

{{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

{{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

{{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

{{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

{{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

{{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.

Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kamo no Tadayuki]][[en:draft:Kamo no Tadayuki]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kamo no Tadayuki|Kamo no Tadayuki]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |Image = |Caption = |Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}} |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|ja|朱雀天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|ja|村上天皇}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Onmyōdō}}

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽家}} active during the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} to {{ill|Mid Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄}}. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|divination|ja|陰陽道}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|lt=Onmyōdō proper|ja|陰陽道}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|calendar scholar|ja|暦家}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Daoist|ja|道教}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Esoteric Buddhism|ja|密教}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|ja|百鬼夜行}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: - ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|ja|筒井康隆}}. - ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yūsuke Shirai|ja|白井悠介}}.
