{{Infobox noble |Name = Kamo no Tadayuki |image = |Caption = |Period = [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]] to [[:en:Heian period|Mid Heian period]] |Birth Date = Unknown |Death Date = Unknown<ref>Some sources suggest the 4th year of Tentoku (960).</ref> |Other Names = |Posthumous Name = |Divine Name = |Religious Name = |Tomb = |Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}; {{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Lord = [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]] → [[:en:Emperor Suzaku|Emperor Suzaku]] → [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hitomaro|1=Kamo-no-Hitomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q77810650}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kei no Hoson|1=Yoshishige no Yasutane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9383388}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:School of Naturalists|Onmyōji]] active during the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]] to [[:en:Heian period|Mid Heian period]]. He is best known as the mentor of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He held the court Rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of [[:en:Onmyōdō|divination]], earning unwavering trust from the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]], describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendar-making|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}, and [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō proper]]—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|Rekidō|lt=Rekidō|lt=Calendar scholar|ja|暦家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦道}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the [[:en:Japanese missions to Tang China|Kentōshi]] missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending [[:en:Taoism|Daoist]] rituals with elements of [[:en:Mikkyō|Esoteric Buddhism]]. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with [[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]] and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: ** ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|1=Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658268}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by [[:en:Yasutaka Tsutsui|Yasutaka Tsutsui]]. ** ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neko Neko Nihonshi|1=Neko Neko Nihonshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873669}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by [[:en:Yusuke Shirai|Yūsuke Shirai]].

The suspension of the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}} was being imported from the continent. '''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble}} and {{ill|School of Naturalists|en|School of Naturalists|WD=Q1195540|lt=Onmyōji}} active during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Early Heian period}} to {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Mid Heian period}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He held the court Rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=divination}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendar-making|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō proper}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|Rekidō|lt=Rekidō|lt=Calendar scholar|ja|暦家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦道}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Daoist}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|en|Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q1358278|lt=Hyakki Yagyō}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: ** ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|1=Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658268}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|en|Yasutaka Tsutsui|WD=Q719250|lt=Yasutaka Tsutsui}}. ** ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neko Neko Nihonshi|1=Neko Neko Nihonshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873669}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yusuke Shirai|en|Yusuke Shirai|WD=Q11579372|lt=Yūsuke Shirai}}.

{{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|en|Emperor Suzaku|WD=Q361019|lt=Emperor Suzaku}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hitomaro|1=Kamo-no-Hitomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q77810650}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kei no Hoson|1=Yoshishige no Yasutane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9383388}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble}} and {{ill|School of Naturalists|en|School of Naturalists|WD=Q1195540|lt=Onmyōji}} active during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Early Heian period}} to {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Mid Heian period}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He held the court Rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=divination}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendar-making|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō proper}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|Rekidō|lt=Rekidō|lt=Calendar scholar|ja|暦家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦道}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Daoist}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|en|Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q1358278|lt=Hyakki Yagyō}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: ** ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|1=Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658268}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|en|Yasutaka Tsutsui|WD=Q719250|lt=Yasutaka Tsutsui}}. ** ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neko Neko Nihonshi|1=Neko Neko Nihonshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873669}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yusuke Shirai|en|Yusuke Shirai|WD=Q11579372|lt=Yūsuke Shirai}}.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

{{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|en|Emperor Suzaku|WD=Q361019|lt=Emperor Suzaku}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hitomaro|1=Kamo-no-Hitomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q77810650}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kei no Hoson|1=Yoshishige no Yasutane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9383388}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble}} and {{ill|School of Naturalists|en|School of Naturalists|WD=Q1195540|lt=Onmyōji}} active during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Early Heian period}} to {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Mid Heian period}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He held the court Rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=divination}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendar-making|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō proper}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|Rekidō|lt=Rekidō|lt=Calendar scholar|ja|暦家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦道}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Daoist}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|en|Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q1358278|lt=Hyakki Yagyō}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: ** ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|1=Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658268}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|en|Yasutaka Tsutsui|WD=Q719250|lt=Yasutaka Tsutsui}}. ** ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neko Neko Nihonshi|1=Neko Neko Nihonshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873669}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yusuke Shirai|en|Yusuke Shirai|WD=Q11579372|lt=Yūsuke Shirai}}.

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|School of Naturalists|en|School of Naturalists|WD=Q1195540|lt=Onmyōji|1=School of Naturalists}} active during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Early Heian period|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Mid Heian period|1=Heian period}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He held the court Rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=divination|1=Onmyōdō}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari|1=Konjaku Monogatarishū}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendar-making|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō proper|1=Onmyōdō}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|Rekidō|lt=Rekidō|lt=Calendar scholar|ja|暦家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦道}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi|1=Japanese missions to Tang China}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Daoist|1=Taoism}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|en|Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q1358278|lt=Hyakki Yagyō|1=Hyakki Yagyō}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: ** ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|1=Q11658268|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658268}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|en|Yasutaka Tsutsui|WD=Q719250|lt=Yasutaka Tsutsui|1=Yasutaka Tsutsui}}. ** ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neko Neko Nihonshi|1=Q24873669|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873669}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yusuke Shirai|en|Yusuke Shirai|WD=Q11579372|lt=Yūsuke Shirai|1=Yusuke Shirai}}.

{{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo|1=Emperor Daigo}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|en|Emperor Suzaku|WD=Q361019|lt=Emperor Suzaku|1=Emperor Suzaku}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hitomaro|1=Kamo-no-Hitomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q77810650}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kei no Hoson|1=Yoshishige no Yasutane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9383388}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|School of Naturalists|en|School of Naturalists|WD=Q1195540|lt=Onmyōji|1=School of Naturalists}} active during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Early Heian period|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Mid Heian period|1=Heian period}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He held the court Rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=divination|1=Onmyōdō}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari|1=Konjaku Monogatarishū}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendar-making|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō proper|1=Onmyōdō}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|Rekidō|lt=Rekidō|lt=Calendar scholar|ja|暦家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦道}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi|1=Japanese missions to Tang China}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Daoist|1=Taoism}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|en|Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q1358278|lt=Hyakki Yagyō|1=Hyakki Yagyō}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: ** ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|1=Q11658268|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658268}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|en|Yasutaka Tsutsui|WD=Q719250|lt=Yasutaka Tsutsui|1=Yasutaka Tsutsui}}. ** ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neko Neko Nihonshi|1=Q24873669|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873669}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yusuke Shirai|en|Yusuke Shirai|WD=Q11579372|lt=Yūsuke Shirai|1=Yusuke Shirai}}.

{{ill|Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja|丹波権介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Tanba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Lord = {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo|1=Emperor Daigo}} → {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|en|Emperor Suzaku|WD=Q361019|lt=Emperor Suzaku|1=Emperor Suzaku}} → {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}} |Clan = {{ill|Kamo Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} |Parents = {{Unbulleted list|Grandfather: {{ill|Kamo no Hitomaro|ja|賀茂人麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Hitomaro|1=Kamo-no-Hitomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q77810650}}|Father: {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="a">{{Cite book|title=Sonpi Bunmyaku|editor=Kamo clan genealogies}}</ref> or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="b">Genealogies in {{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}</ref>}} |Siblings = |Spouse = |Children = {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}}, Kamo no Hogen, {{ill|Kei no Hoson|ja|慶滋保胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kei no Hoson|1=Yoshishige no Yasutane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9383388}}, Kei no Hojo |Significant Achievements = }}
'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|School of Naturalists|en|School of Naturalists|WD=Q1195540|lt=Onmyōji|1=School of Naturalists}} active during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Early Heian period|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Mid Heian period|1=Heian period}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He held the court Rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=divination|1=Onmyōdō}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari|1=Konjaku Monogatarishū}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendar-making|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō proper|1=Onmyōdō}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|Rekidō|lt=Rekidō|lt=Calendar scholar|ja|暦家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦道}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|WD=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi|1=Japanese missions to Tang China}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|WD=Q9598|lt=Daoist|1=Taoism}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|WD=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|en|Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q1358278|lt=Hyakki Yagyō|1=Hyakki Yagyō}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: ** ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|1=Q11658268|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658268}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|en|Yasutaka Tsutsui|WD=Q719250|lt=Yasutaka Tsutsui|1=Yasutaka Tsutsui}}. ** ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neko Neko Nihonshi|1=Q24873669|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24873669}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yusuke Shirai|en|Yusuke Shirai|WD=Q11579372|lt=Yūsuke Shirai|1=Yusuke Shirai}}.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Onmyōdō}}

'''Kamo no Tadayuki''' (賀茂 忠行) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|School of Naturalists|en|School of Naturalists|qid=Q1195540|lt=Onmyōji|1=School of Naturalists}} active during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Early Heian period|1=Heian period}} to {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Mid Heian period|1=Heian period}}. He is best known as the mentor of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|qid=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. His father was either {{ill|Kamo no Enjin|ja|賀茂江人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Enjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Kamo no Mineo|ja|賀茂峯雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Mineo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He held the court Rank of Junior Fifth Rank and served as Assistant Governor of Tanba.
== Achievements ==
Kamo no Tadayuki excelled in the art of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=divination|1=Onmyōdō}}, earning unwavering trust from the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. He was particularly renowned for his skill in ''{{ill|Shikuku|ja|射覆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (divining the contents of a concealed object). One notable incident, recorded in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|qid=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari|1=Konjaku Monogatarishū}}, describes how he correctly identified a crystal rosary wrapped in red cord hidden in an octagonal box, astonishing the emperor and earning widespread acclaim.
== Role in Onmyōdō History ==
Tadayuki's contributions to {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} were pivotal. Prior to his time, the three branches of Onmyōdō—{{ill|Astronomy|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Calendar-making|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendar-making|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}, and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō proper|1=Onmyōdō}}—were practiced separately by specialized experts. However, Tadayuki, originally a {{ill|Rekidō|lt=Rekidō|lt=Calendar scholar|ja|暦家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar scholar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦道}}, transcended these divisions, uniting the disciplines under the leadership of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}. This consolidation laid the groundwork for the future dominance of the Kamo and {{ill|Abe clan|ja|安倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} in Onmyōdō.
== Historical Context ==
The suspension of the {{ill|Japanese missions to Tang China|en|Japanese missions to Tang China|qid=Q262529|lt=Kentōshi|1=Japanese missions to Tang China}} missions in the 6th year of Kanpyō (894) under {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} meant that no new knowledge on {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}} was being imported from the continent. This restriction fostered an environment where Onmyōdō practices became increasingly reliant on existing knowledge. The Kamo clan took advantage of this situation to innovate, blending {{ill|Taoism|en|Taoism|qid=Q9598|lt=Daoist|1=Taoism}} rituals with elements of {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|qid=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}. This shift from purely technical practices to religious and ritualistic functions revitalized the declining discipline.
== Appearances in Literature and Media ==
Tadayuki is featured prominently in legends such as: * ''Konjaku Monogatari'': Narratives detail his encounters with {{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|en|Hyakki Yagyō|qid=Q1358278|lt=Hyakki Yagyō|1=Hyakki Yagyō}} and his mentorship of Abe no Seimei. * He is depicted in modern adaptations such as: ** ''{{ill|Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|ja|陰陽師☆安倍晴明〜王都妖奇譚〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji☆Abe Seimei: Royal Capital Mystical Tales|1=Q11658268|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658268}}'' (2002, Fuji TV), played by {{ill|Yasutaka Tsutsui|en|Yasutaka Tsutsui|qid=Q719250|lt=Yasutaka Tsutsui|1=Yasutaka Tsutsui}}. ** ''{{ill|Neko Neko Nihonshi|ja|ねこねこ日本史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Neko Neko Nihonshi|1=Q24873669|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24873669}}'' (2018, NHK), voiced by {{ill|Yusuke Shirai|en|Yusuke Shirai|qid=Q11579372|lt=Yūsuke Shirai|1=Yusuke Shirai}}.
